data:
This page holds reference to all of the main characters of the story:
Vesper Treadway Syren Ashryver Aelyra Caladwen Elowen Giggleleaf
Played by Alex
data.backstory#Backstory: Aelyra Caladwen is the daughter of Emporer Caladwen of the Twilight Court. As such, she grew up wealthy and well-off, but she has a rebellious streak in her that couldn’t easily be controlled. She has always had a lust for adventure, a love for music, a compassion for the less fortunate, and the drive to break free from the golden chains that had bound her. When Syren suggested to her best friend Aelyra that she wanted to leave the court in search of her sister, Aelyra jumped at the opportunity.
data.character-traits#Character Traits: Lordly, upper-eschelon mannerisms “Do you know who I am?” Skilled musician Despite growing up wealthy and powerful, feels a connection to common and working people
data.relationships#Relationships: Close friends with Syren, grew up together in the High Court Complicated relationship with her parents, neither of whom really ever understood her indifference towards the crown

data: Played by Bri
data.backstory#Backstory: Little is known about the past of the mysterious Elowen. She simply wanders the world, helping whoever she can however she can, and following the wind. She is odd and whimsical; dreamy yet perceptive; a gentle healer and lover of nature.
data.character-traits#Character Traits: Compassionate Cares for ALL living things, no matter their nature
data.motivations#Motivations: To protect those who need protecting To be one with nature
data.relationships#Relationships: The wind seems to be a pretty tight homie tbh

data: Played by Justine
data.backstory#Backstory: Syren grew up in The Twilight Court of Squawhorn under a strict military general father. Her mother fell in love with a human (ew) and ran off from the court, leaving Syren motherless and bitter. She was fully expected to engage in military service, herself, but the discovery that she had a long-lost half-sister sparked the fire of a personal quest inside her. She must find the half-sister. When she told her lifelong friend Aelyra of her plot, her best friend agreed to accompany her, and together, they embarked on a quest east towards Estharia, the direction her mother traveled so many years before.
data.character-traits#Character Traits: She’s, like, really strong Gruff and intimidating A little bloodthirsty sometimes Protective of Aelyra
data.relationships#Relationships: Close friends with Aelyra, grew up together in the High Court Complicated relationship with her parents: Father was an overbearing military man, mother left when she was young.

data: Played by Brittney
data.backstory#Backstory:
From Brittney:
She’s a half elf rogue who performs lute in taverns while quietly relieving wealthy patrons of their coin purses. But don’t worry, she’s not a monster. She will leave behind chocolate coins and place of stolen ones. She’s charming, a little chaotic, and extremely passionate about collecting beautiful daggers. She insists her good luck comes from a tiny, invisible duck named marzipan, which she assures everyone is completely real.
Vesper Treadway grew up in Ratwhistle with her adoring human Father, from whom she adopted her affinity for knives.
data.character-traits#Character Traits: Self-Assured Standoffish and independent Of questionable morality (but “not a monster” as she says) Might be crazy with the whole duck thing, maybe?
data.marzipan#Marzipan: Marzipan is an invisible, potentially imaginary Duck to whom Vesper believes she owes her life after an incident in Ratwhistle during her childhood that nearly took her life. This duck provides much needed companionship to Vesper, who travels alone, and Vesper frequently consults her feathered friend for advice on risky situations. Is Marzipan real? Time will tell. Time… Will… Tell…
data.relationships#Relationships: A close relationship with her father Marzipan is a real one for sure

data:
All of the details regarding the NPCs will be here, with some general sorting.
Humanoids Demons Imps

This section holds reference to all of the demon characters that have been encountered by the player characters.
[Belvedere](./belvedere

Belvedere was Findlay’s personal servant, enslaved by a demonic contract. He was freed in Session #1 by Findlay after he had accidentally spilled tea on the demonic contracts, but then took power over the imps personally. He was killed by the player characters at the end of the session, freeing the imps and allowing them to return home to The Abyss.

This section is specifically for Humanoid characters, like Humans and Elves and Dwarves.
[Findlay](./findlay [Emporer Caladwen](./emporer-caladwen [General Ashryver](./general-ashryver
Emporer Caladwen is the emporer of Squawhorn, who sits the thrown of The Twilight Court. He is the father to Aelrya Caladwen.

Findlay is the owner of the Lamp Emporium in Norville. He had enslaved the demon Belvedere as his personal servent as well as several Imps as his workers, until he accidentally destroyed his demonic contracts in Session #1, freeing the demon and imps from their bonds. The players freed the imps and implored Findlay to hire paid workers in the future instead of meddling with the enslavement of demons and imps.

General Ashryver is the top general of Squawhorn, who regularly counsils with The Twilight Court and serves as its military arm. He is the father to Syren Ashryver.
Might be dead? Unclear, but Justine did say that he was dead.

This section holds reference to all of the imp characters that have been encountered by the player characters:
Max Biff [Fog](./fog [Yuck](yuck
Biff is Max’s right-hand imp. He is gruff, with a thick cockney-like accent. He fights with a club and don’t take no guff from nobody, I tells ‘ya.
Fog is one of Max’s imp followers. He is a small, grey imp with slight demonic traits. He has a New York accent and breathes plumes of smoke.

Max is the defacto leader of the imps, popular among most of them and has earned their loyalty through his own deeds. He is brown and looks quite a bit like a bat. He has been described as “very cool”. His right-hand man, best friend, and most trusted ally among the imps is Biff.
Yuck is one of the imps loyal to [Belvedere](../../demons/belvedere. He was a slender pink cat-like imp. Vesper cut off his tail before Syren slit his throat and killed her in the first session.

This is where all of the setting details will go, details that we’ve learned about the places we’ve come up with, where they are, the people and places there, etc. Organized by country.
Estharia Squawhorn
Norville is the country in which the story begins and primarily takes place. It neighbors Squawhorn.

Norville is a mid-sized trading town where travelers frequently come and go. Adventurers looking for a place to rest, merchants looking to trade, and craftspeople looking for short-term work frequently come and go.
data.findlays-lamp-emporium#Findlay\u0026rsqu:
Findlay’s Lamp Emporium is a tall, narrow, Weasley-house-esque building that is exactly what it sounds like. Findlay and his imp servants produce all manner of lamps and lamp-like objects, some of which are magical in nature. The Lamp Emporium is host to a direct portal to The Abyss, the demonic realm from whence the imps and demons came. A magical welcome mat embued with the words “Not Welcome” keeps the demons from coming into the material plane from The Abyss.
In the first session of the game, the lamp emporium is the focal point of the story. Findlay had enslaved a demon and several imps to do his bidding in the lamp shop, both personally and professionally, but he accidentally spilled tea on his demonic contracts, freeing them from their magical bindings, and they quickly overthrew Findlay and removed him from power. The player characters ultimately decided to free the imps from both Findlay’s control as well as Belvedere’s.
data.the-lute--lyre#The Lute \u0026amp; Lyre: The Lute & Lyre is a quiet tavern located in Norville. It’s run by an angel of a woman, similar to Grandma Rocky, and is frequented by day drunks and bar flies. It’s notable for having a large stage for performances, which are put to use somewhat regularly and without much schedule.

data: Ratwhistle is the hometown of Vesper Treadway. It’s a small, quaint village, mostly inhabited by farmers who grow and tend to the farmlands, and a small band of thieves who look out for each other. They steal from other towns and bring the spoils back to Ratwhistle to share with the residents, including those who do not partake in thievery.

Squawhorn is a neighboring country to Estharia. It is a wealthy, war-waging nation and home to The Twilight Court. [Syren Ashriver](../../../main-characters/syren-ashryver and [Aelyra Caladwen](../../../main-characters/aelyra-caladwen come from this nation.
[The Twilight Court](the-twilight-court
The Twilight Court is the seat of the high council of Squawhorn, the nation from which Aelyra Caladwen and Syren Ashryver hail. It is ruled by Emporer Caladwen.

This section contains all of the session summaries. Each session summary will contain a short summary at the top, followed by a more detailed account of the story, and finally followed by the rewards that came from that session.
[Session 1: The Lamp Emporium](s001-the-lamp-shop
This section is currently incomplete!
data.breaking-into-the-lamp-emporium#Breaking:
The players follow Findlay back to the Lamp Emporium. Findlay points to a mark on the ground and says, “I can’t get any closer than that to the building.” The players test the ward, ensuring that they are not also kept out by the magic. They are not, and so Vesper approaches the primary entrance which has a poorly written and heavily mispelled sign that says, “Closed. Reopening under new management. -B”. She tries the door, but finds it locked, and so she produces her thieves kit and picks the lock, which is a little more complex than she’s accustomed to, but she gets it unlocked without getting anyone’s attention and takes a peek inside to scope out the situation.
Peering through the door, Vesper can see three imps on the floor of a well-stocked lamp emporium show room. Two of them–one, a skunk-like imp with two parallel stripes running along its back, and the other a small gray demon with enormous pointed ears and a thin tail with a sharp barb at the end of it–are painting a new sign for the lamp emporium. The third, a slightly bigger but still relatively small green creature, paced back and forth with a club, overseeing the sign, nodding with approval at the mispelled words and malformed letters.
Vesper returns to the party to tell them what she saw, and she suggests a silent approach. Syren expresses that she feels confident that she would walk in and kill the imps with no need for secrecy, stating that she is “really powerful”. Aelyra suggests that, perhaps they don’t kill the cute little guys, but instead, perhaps they could persuade them, and she even suggests using some of her magic to do it. Vesper seems to like that plan, suggesting that they try to simply buy a lamp, or ask for a tour of the lamp emporium. “And then kill them?” Syren suggests, and Aelyra says, “No no no… I think that these imps deserve to live.” They start to think about Findlay and his motives, starting to doubt whether they can trust someone who enslaved these creatures, and consider helping the imps instead.
After a discussion about what to do with the imps and Findlay, the party decides to side with the imps and to secure their freedom fully, finding the enslavement of the small infernal creatures to be morally abhorrent, despite their impish nature. The plot consists of simply entering the Lamp Emporium and offering to help Belvedere secure the Lamp Emporium and to help keep Findlay at bay, for a price. Syren offers several times to kill Findlay, to the objections of the rest of the group. Aelyra wants to ease into the situation with a little bit of magical assistance, so she plans to discretely cast Charm Person on the bigger green imp prior to entering the room.
And so they do that. Aelyra very quietly opens the door, reaches her hand inside, and casts Charm person towards the green imp. The spell binds successfully, but the imps all notice the spell being cast from across the room. The larger imp takes no action, being under Aelyra’s spell, but the other two imps take action immediately. They drop their paintbrushes and one of the imps–a small, grey one–opens his mouth and breathes out enormous plumes of smoke, filling the room. Aelyrca shouts through the smoke and fog at the imp under her spell, telling him they just want to talk, and he calms his fellow imps down and asks what she wants. Aelyra explains the proposition to the imp, named [Biff](../../npcs/imps/biff, who quietly expresses disdain towards Belvedere and says, “You want to help us? I got someone else you should talk to, come with me.” Aelyra calls the rest of her party in and they follow the imp up the stairs.
The second floor is a workshop, with many various lamps in progress, a kiln, and some imps making lamp s there. A small bat-like imp is also working, and that is who Biff takes them to. He says, “[Max](../../npcs/imps/max, these people want to talk to Belvedere”, to which Max replies, “Oh… Fuck Belvedere. What do you want with him?” Max reveals that Belvedere is powerful, and wants to crush all of Norville, but the Imps just want to return to their native plane and want no part in Belvedere’s overarching evil plan, but when Findlay nulled their contracts, Belvedere immediately bound the impish contracts under his control. The players are reluctant, and ask for a reward to help them, to which Max says that they have no money, but they can have whatever is in the lamp emporium that they want. Vesper asks for their tails in exchange instead, and Max immediately agrees to cut his tail off and says that it’s a small price to pay, though he also tells the players that not all of the imps are on board with overthrowing Belvedere.
While this discussion is happening, Syren and Vesper look around to see which imps in the room are in agreement and realize that one of the imps–a small, adorable pink imp named [Yuck](../../npcs/imps/yuck–is trying to sneak up the stairs to the next floor away from them, and they both sprint after him to bring him back. Syren trips and falls, still feeling the liquor in her belly. Vesper leaps over the prone Syren and grabs the imp by the scruff of the neck, pulling it back into the room with the rest of them. The pink imp begins yelling about how Belvedere will not let Max or the elves get away with their plot against him, and that he’s powerful.
Syren, still on the floor, says, “I have a lot of power.”
Vesper begins interrogating the pink imp, wanting to know Belvedere’s weaknesses and how to find and defeat him, but Yuck is defiant and spits acid at her. Vesper, frustrated with the stubbornness, cuts off the imp’s tail and wraps it around her neck like a scarf. After a lot of fruitless (but very spit-ful) back and forth between Yuck and Vesper, Syren steps in and begins taking fingers. Yuck caves and tells the players that Belvedere loves impish bon-bons. After getting the information from Yuck, Syren slits Yuck’s throat, killing him.
data.highlights#Highlights: The characters all meet in the Norville tavern, The Lute & Lyre, and prepare for a performance. During Vesper and Aelyra’s performance, a man bursts through the door pleading for help He is the owner of the Lamp Emporium, and tells everyone that he spilled tea on his demonic contracts that bound a demon and several imps to his servitude, which voided the contracts and freed the demon and imps. He’s lost controls of his shop and needs help breaking the ward, killing the infernal creatures, and getting back into his shop and home. The characters agree to help! They head to the lamp emporium and Vesper picks the lock of the main entrance They find some imps on the showroom floor painting a sign, planning to open the shop the following day Having a change of heart and considering the nature of the enslavement, the players decide to instead help Belvedere retain control of the lamp emporium for a price. Aelyra casts Charm person on one of the imps (Biff, who takes them to their de facto leader [Max](../../npcs/imps/max, who is planning a rebellion against the Demon [Belvedere](../../npcs/demons/belvedere, who has reinstated the contracts for himself. After a discussion with Max regarding Belvedere’s desires to destroy Norville, the party has another change of heart and decides to kill Belvedere for the imps, freeing them of their binding contracts.
data.the-lute--lyre#The Lute \u0026amp; Lyre:
The story starts in the mid-sized trade town of Norville. It’s a wet, cold, stinky, rainy spring day, some time between 2 and 4 pm, maybe 3 pm, who’s to say, live your own truth or whatever. Four adventurers are coincidentally drawn to Norville for one reason or another.
Elowen Giggleleaf, the mysterious druid (NOT cleric, thank you very much) was drawn to Norville by the wind, which told her that a beautiful white horse had an injury and needed her help. She found the horse behind the local tavern called The Lute & Lyre, lying in a stable, injured. Drawing upon her earthly magic, she healed the horse, who gave her a nod and a wink as if to say, “thanks bro, good lookin’ out”.
Vesper Treadway arrived at 3:00 pm as is her own truth and entered the Lute & Lyre. She’s been here before, and she’ll be back again. She begins to set up to perform, but has arrived a bit early. The tavern is dead, save for a few poor souls already drunk. The coffers are clearly running dry among the patrons here. Even still, the day is early, and she hopes that later on, more people start filing in.
Aelyra Caladwen and Syren Ashryver arrive shortly after Vesper, with the specific intent of Aelyra performing. Aelyra immediately takes note of the stage AND the person on it and immediately thinks, “Oh, her and I could play together, cool!” Aelyra approaches Vesper, who is preparing the space for her performance, and says, “Hey, I see you got an instrument. Yeah. I also have an instrument. Where are you gonna play that instrument? I was gonna do a little ditty.” She gestures towareds Syren. “This is my friend. She doesn’t have instruments, but she’s cool. She’s here… She’s here for the vibes, okay? And I need money, too.”
Syren injerjects on Aelyra’s behalf while Aelyra tunes her lyre (a.k.a. baby harp). She invoked Aelyra’s status as high nobility of a neighboring country, but Vesper is unimpressed by the credentials. Aelyra begins playing a beautiful quiet ballad-type of song, invoking tears in the eyes of the drunk men all around. Vesper watches, largely unimpressed though silently acknowledging some amount of talent there, straps on her Lute and begins playing Bon Jovi’s “Livin’ on a Prayer”, which also invokes tears. Vesper agrees to allow Aelyra to play as backup, but must follow Vesper’s lead. Aelyra agrees, reluctantly.
The characters spend the next few hours “getting hammer drunk” and waiting for more patrons to file in before performing. Hours later, when the tavern finally appears to be sufficiently populated, the musicians take the stage and begin playing what may or may not have been Bob Marley’s “Jammin’”, and the patrons love it. They’re eating it up.
Mere moments into the performance, a man bursts through the tavern door in a panic, shouting for help in a panic. Vesper vaguely recognizes the man as the owner of the Lamp Emporium, and she and Aelyra stop playing to listen to what he has to say. He notices that he has the attention of the people nearby and continues, rubbing his cheeks with his palms nervously:
I’ve spilled tea all over my demonic contracts at the at the lamp shop and now the demon and all the imps that I had serving me are freed and I don’t know what to do. They rebelled and kicked me out of the lamp emporium. I can’t get back in. They’re running the place, Oh, I need to get back in there, I have important blueprints and my spellbook, they have everything. [Belvedere](../../npcs/demons/belvedere, my demon servant, took my finger and is using it to bind a ward that keeps me from being able to enter! Are any of you adventureres that can help me? I can offer many rewards!
Aelyra immediately agrees to help, and as a result of that, Syren also agrees to help, but only to help Aelyra and keep her out of trouble. Elowen, who has been silently observing from the corner, emerges and also agrees to assist, insisting that she wants to ensure that nobody gets hurt. Vesper expresses some reluctance, and asks, “Well, what’s in it for me?”
Findlay lays out his terms. The Imps must be dealt with, and for every imp tail brought to him, he will pay four gold. For one of Belvedere’s horns, he will also pay, and he will also pay if Belvedere is demonstrably killed or banished from the natural plane. He will pay for the return of his finger, for the breaking of the ward, for the discovery and return of his spellbook, and for a blueprint for a special lamp that he is working on. Elowen asks, “Can I have a lamp?” to which Findlay agrees unconditionally. Vesper decides to join, but asks Findlay to watch her lute, to which he also agrees.
